items.q: What is the 'nosotros' form of 'hablar' (to speak)?
items.why: Students confuse nosotros (we) with ellos (they). AR verbs in present: hablo, hablas, habla, HABLAMOS, habláis, hablan. The nosotros ending is -amos, not -an.

A notorious faux ami that causes real confusion. 'Estoy embarazada' = 'I am pregnant'. For 'embarrassed' use 'avergonzado/a'. Native speakers laugh at this one - learners avoid by association with shame.

items.q: How do you say 'I like coffee' (in general) in Spanish?
items.why: English drops the article for general likes; Spanish keeps it. For general preferences, use the DEFINITE article: me gusta EL chocolate, me gustan LOS libros.
It's Lifestyle. “Free time” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.
It's Sin embargo. “Ademas” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: How many prescribed themes in MFL Language B?
items.why: Exact answer: 5. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
